burn_scar_fused.evidence: S2=no_data; Landsat=no_data; S1 dVV=-0.033dB; MODIS-SR=no_data; MODIS-AF=no_data

vision.basis: The top-right portion shows a bright cyan-white feature consistent with shallow water or salt deposits. The vivid blue signature in the Sentinel-2 false-color composite (SWIR-NIR channels) is characteristic of water bodies. No evidence of burn scars, thermal persistence, or human infrastructure.

burn_scar_fused.evidence: S2=no_data; Landsat dNBR_max=0.2439 (low); S1 dVV=-0.112dB; MODIS-SR=no_data; MODIS-AF=no_data

vision.basis: The visible imagery shows a dark rectangular array with regular grid pattern characteristic of solar panels. The Sentinel-2 false-color composite displays the distinctive dark grey/black signature typical of photovoltaic installations, with no evidence of burn scars or vegetation damage.
